MeshCore
📚 Forums / software-development / Meshcore Open App
RocketMan 2026-01-01 5:56 p.m.
I’ve been working on meshcore-open, an MIT open-source MeshCore client, and I wanted to share what it currently does in case it’s useful or interesting to others here. At a high level, it’s a local-first MeshCore interface that lets you interact with the mesh without relying on the internet. It supports encrypted direct messages and public channels, keeps local conversation history, and tracks contacts with last-seen timestamps. Messages support replies and reactions so conversations are easier to follow on busy meshes. On the mesh side, you can see routing paths, and how messages move through the network. Routes can rotate automatically or be overridden manually, and nearby nodes are discovered dynamically. There’s also a map view that shows live node locations when available, with filtering by node type and time range. You can share GPS locations or custom markers, use offline map tiles in remote areas. For device interaction, it connects over BLE (NUS) to MeshCore radios, allowing you to configure radio parameters, power settings, and network options, monitor battery state with chemistry-aware voltage curves. If you’re running repeaters, there’s a repeater hub that exposes stats, connected clients, traffic data, and remote management tools, including CLI access over the mesh itself. It’s still evolving and definitely has some rough edges, but the goal is to make an open source app that every one can freely use and modify. Feedback is welcome https://github.com/zjs81/meshcore-open
GitHub - zjs81/meshcore-open: Open-source Flutter client for MeshCo...
Open-source Flutter client for MeshCore LoRa mesh networking devices - zjs81/meshcore-open

I’ve just compiled it on MacOS and wanted to pass along some quick observations -
  • not possible to copy/text contents of a message
  • hyper links not clickable I haven’t compiled the iOS build yet so I’m not sure if this is specific to iOS. Overall I’m impressed with it - it shows a lot of promise. Jude 2026-01-03 10:16 p.m.
With the gifs being sent over the mesh does it just send an id to a image to the gif online or does it send the full gif?
Jude
With the gifs being sent over the mesh does it just send an id to a image to the gif online or does it send the full gif?
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-03 10:24 p.m.
It posts the unique ID from giphy for the gif. On the mesh, this got posted: g:7xkxbhryQO7hm The client unrolls it to: https://giphy.com/gifs/7xkxbhryQO7hm Great way to save bandwidth
3
paul / BostonMesh
It posts the unique ID from giphy for the gif. On the mesh, this got posted: g:7xkxbhryQO7hm The client unrolls it to: https://giphy.com/gifs/7xkxbhryQO7hm Great way to save bandwidth
Jude 2026-01-03 10:25 p.m.
Ohhh cool, would be interesting if we could send the whole gif over the mesh but this is probably a way better way to do it
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-03 10:36 p.m.
It requires internet access to unroll, but if the client can detect that it is offline, it can probably hide messages that are in the "g:[unique-id]" format.

SMAZ?
RocketMan 2026-01-03 11:36 p.m.
Smaz is text compression you can click the i while in dms to enable it or edit the channel to enable it. It reduces msg size by up to 50%

RocketMan 2026-01-04 12:42 a.m.
I will also be implementing an open source MQTT analyzer into it that uses node authentication to auth sharing data. In my state at least aggregating location data of people and data without consent is considered a felony as it could cause fear or harm to people if the platform has a potential of exposing this even if it is being broadcast as this requires explicit consent. If there are children involved it could be even worse. Basically how it will work is the nodes that want to share data will have to create a shared secret with the server using the servers public key and their private key just like how dms work which will act as a consent hash. But this is why meshtastic has the ok to MQTT setting.
2
1
12:43 a.m.
This will be able to be hosted by anyone.
12:43 a.m.
People will say well they could log that data anyway but to that I say I gave them the tools if they get in trouble thats on them

Erik S56EK 2026-01-05 3:23 p.m.
this would be better if implemented in the companion FW IMHO. "toggle" a switch from the app and the tracker should send location and battery data in a smart way - meaning, if the person or DOG is not moving, there is no need to send location data (to conserve battery) etc.
Erik S56EK
this would be better if implemented in the companion FW IMHO. "toggle" a switch from the app and the tracker should send location and battery data in a smart way - meaning, if the person or DOG is not moving, there is no need to send location data (to conserve battery) etc.
RocketMan 2026-01-05 5:33 p.m.
I dont think it would be. Gotta remeber these things have limited compute power and are mostly single threaded. This same smart feature could be done on the app. Also, its really hard to get anything pr'd into the firmware in my experience anyway
paul / BostonMesh
@RocketMan quick thought.. “yt:[youtube-unique-identifier]”
RocketMan 2026-01-05 5:34 p.m.
Good idea im thinking about link safety atm among other things so it will be in the next version
RocketMan
I dont think it would be. Gotta remeber these things have limited compute power and are mostly single threaded. This same smart feature could be done on the app. Also, its really hard to get anything pr'd into the firmware in my experience anyway
Erik S56EK 2026-01-05 5:36 p.m.
Yes, I know, but it’s already working (check the open source (should be) Meshcore SAR). The problem relaying on an app to send the location becames a problem if the app looses BT connection.

RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:39 p.m.
Hey guys sorry for the delay. I got the test flight stuff paid for still working on getting it up though.
11:39 p.m.
I've been seeing that people are starting to crack hash tag channels. To counter act this im going to be adding a community mode to the app that will allow for the creation of community hashtag channels and a community public channel. This will use a shared secret qr code shared in the community which will then use hmac to generate the hashes thus stopping people from doing this.
realtag 2026-01-18 11:45 p.m.
but those channels are meant to be easily decryptable by generating the same key based on hashtag. unless I am misunderstanding it
11:45 p.m.
@RocketMan
3
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:47 p.m.
They are but with the advent of traffic being uploaded to the internet my mesh prefers to keep stuff in the community. Regular hashtag channels will still exist. This is just to make things more annoying for outsiders to look in.
11:48 p.m.
So basically if you wanna see the mesh messages you gotta be on the mesh
1
realtag 2026-01-18 11:50 p.m.
so you basically want to create a way for people to share a secret room hash/password ?
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:50 p.m.
Yep
realtag 2026-01-18 11:50 p.m.
similar to what they have on MT ?
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:50 p.m.
Its not too crazy
realtag
similar to what they have on MT ?
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:51 p.m.
Im not aware of what they do on MT
realtag 2026-01-18 11:51 p.m.
they have a qr code that just adds a private room
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:51 p.m.
Oh
realtag 2026-01-18 11:51 p.m.
two people do the same and they can chat privately
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:52 p.m.
Basically the community would post a qr code youd scan it then have a community public channel and be able to see and create community hash tag channels
11:52 p.m.
So kinda the same
realtag 2026-01-18 11:52 p.m.
yeah that should be a great addition for a massive mesh
11:52 p.m.
on our small mesh we are barely using hashtags, but thats because public channel is not too busy yet
RocketMan 2026-01-18 11:54 p.m.
My mesh gets lots of traffic and we dont like plain text data aggregation so thats the main reason for us. I can see this being good for lots of reasons. Also the channel names wouldnt collide between communities anymore so more message space technically
11:55 p.m.
I recently got channel creation and such working well in the main branch
11:55 p.m.
You'd have to build from source to see it
11:56 p.m.
But this is the next logical step
11:57 p.m.
We have lots of cool features coming in the next version with more feature parity to the normal app and I should get ios going sometime this week
11:58 p.m.
After we reach feature parity I will be creating a pure telemetry mqtt feature as well for people who like to see metrics
realtag
they have a qr code that just adds a private room
kri5irk 2026-01-19 9:22 a.m.
If you make a private channel in the current MeshCore app you can do the same thing, go to share and there’s a QR code with the secret key

shenntek 2026-01-21 1:31 p.m.
Awesome work on the iOS app! Congrats! One thing I found: when logging into the repeater and view telemetry. I only get “Channel 1” even though I have 5 channels on that repeater setup
1:32 p.m.
Also retrieving TX power stays empty
1:36 p.m.
Sending CLI command via buttons above fills in the text field but the command is already send. So because it fills in the input field you think you still have to send the command via the “>” send button. Which causes the command send twice which could result in this error: Fout: Exception: Another command is still awaiting a response.
shenntek 2026-01-21 1:43 p.m.
Some Dutch translations are weird

PiGuy9614 2026-01-21 4:28 p.m.
I’ve been trying out the TestFlight today, just wanted to say great work!!! I actually started work on a Flutter MeshCore client as well around mid-December, but you well beat me to a finished product (and far more polished than I ever would have gotten mine, might I add!). Great work! I might be sending some minor UI adjustment PRs your way soon, if that’s okay! Thanks a lot for your hard work!
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-21 4:47 p.m.
Wow - TestFlight version is great! I’m curious, how do you trigger a smaz compressed message? I tried to cut/paste long text, but it was truncated.

Love the iOS app, just 1 quirk- when discovering devices, it will not list any, you cancel the search and re-search again and it will find your devices ; When you klick on the channels tab it takes 4-5s to actually load the channels-I have 5of them.

RocketMan 2026-01-21 5:19 p.m.

1
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-21 5:21 p.m.
I love the design decisions - its truly useful information that is great for troubleshooting
2
5:22 p.m.
.. and you pulled this together so quickly.
paul / BostonMesh
.. and you pulled this together so quickly.
RocketMan 2026-01-21 5:23 p.m.
My main flow is i let ai take a stab at it then I fix what it breaks or does manually. After that I define my vars and format code how I want and use the correct widgets and components. Then I have ai pr review and test it then push it
5:23 p.m.
Thats the best way to use ai i think at least for projects that need long term support
5:24 p.m.
My main goal is to make it so people dont want other tools because they have this app
5:25 p.m.
So im happy to hear you like it so far
5:25 p.m.
It still feels kinda rough and there is lots that needs to be done
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-21 5:26 p.m.
I agree - some rough spots here/there, but you are in alpha right now. It’s useable today.
2
RocketMan 2026-01-21 5:26 p.m.
I also want to have reusable components and a fluent ui. Ai wants to create a new menu or component for everything so im trying to stay away from that
5:27 p.m.
Like if you look at meshcore protocol I manually refactoring that after Ai wrote it
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-21 5:28 p.m.
Quick question on the iOS / SMAZ side - I’ve edited the channel to enable it, but am not seeing a way to send long messages to a channel
RocketMan 2026-01-21 5:28 p.m.
https://github.com/zjs81/meshcore-open/pull/16/changes/bc6c1f1fabc36ca88b0a9b29d69a57ecca8bd8da
Added telemetry to repeater management by wel97459 · Pull Request ...
Added telemetry to repeater management: Telemetry is request with a binary request packet, and the returned data is parsed with cayenne_lpp.dart NoteIntroduces binary telemetry across the stack...
paul / BostonMesh
Quick question on the iOS / SMAZ side - I’ve edited the channel to enable it, but am not seeing a way to send long messages to a channel
RocketMan 2026-01-21 5:29 p.m.
It doesnt calculate for the message size just initial bit size but it does reduce msg size by 30 to 50%
5:29 p.m.
So msgs have a higher reliability overall
5:29 p.m.
But it doesnt allow you to send longer msgs
5:30 p.m.
That is something that could be done tho
5:31 p.m.
Its good for situations where you want to send a longer msgs but don't have the best connection (edited)
5:32 p.m.
Id have to make it so it calculates the hash after each key press to make sure you dont go over
5:32 p.m.
Since its not a static size
5:32 p.m.
But the protocol itself has a max size (edited)
1
Meno 2026-01-21 6:29 p.m.
Where i can find the discovered nodes?
2
1
6:32 p.m.

2
1
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-21 7:28 p.m.
@RocketMan quick thought on pulling repeater stats - consider storing the values locally so that you can do some data analysis. I’m logging in to see if battery has gone up/down, check on the neighbors etc. It would be interesting if you added some spark lines to see trends over time. Are the repeaters stronger/weaker/etc. How much has the battery gone up/down, etc. Maybe spark lines for the packets Tx/Rx’d etc. Every other client view the status page as simple point in time display of the data - if the client is pulling it down, automatically storing it would be handy. You could display Tufte-esque spark lines next to the values, or simply have a historical “status” page for all of the data points.
paul / BostonMesh 2026-01-21 7:44 p.m.
Additional idea - allow one to override the local map and display an online map that can be configured. Being able to see our live map (https://live.bostonme.sh/) in app would be rad. Obviously requires internet access though.
Greater Boston Mesh Map
Live view of Greater Boston Mesh MeshCore nodes, message routes, and advert paths.

Just Stuff™ 2026-02-11 9:13 p.m.
Forwarded
I use this to discover repeaters in the Web app. mesh-utility.replit.app function buildNodeDiscoverReq(filter: number): Uint8Array { const tag = Math.floor(Math.random() * 0xFFFFFFFF); const data = new Uint8Array(1 + 1 + 1 + 4); data[0] = CMD_SEND_CONTROL_DATA; data[1] = CONTROL_NODE_DISCOVER_REQ | 0x01; data[2] = filter; data[3] = tag & 0xFF; data[4] = (tag >> 8) & 0xFF; data[5] = (tag >> 16) & 0xFF; data[6] = (tag >> 24) & 0xFF; remoteLog("log", `node_discover cmd: ${Array.from(data).map(b => b.toString(16).padStart(2, "0")).join(" ")}, filter=${filter}, tag=${tag}`); return data; } function parseControlDataFrame(frame: Uint8Array): NodeDiscoverEntry | null { if (frame.length < 5 || frame[0] !== PUSH_CODE_CONTROL_DATA) return null; const view = new DataView(frame.buffer, frame.byteOffset, frame.byteLength); const snrRaw = view.getInt8(1); const snr = snrRaw / 4; const rssi = view.getInt8(2); const pathLen = frame[3]; const payload = frame.slice(4); remoteLog("log", `ControlData raw: snr=${snr} rssi=${rssi} pathLen=${pathLen} payload(${payload.length}b)=${Array.from(payload).map(b => b.toString(16).padStart(2, "0")).join(" ")}`); if (payload.length < 1) { remoteLog("log", `ControlData: empty payload`); return null; }
Originally sent: 2026-02-11 8:45 p.m. (edited)

ded (SalishMesh) 2026-02-11 11:09 p.m.

11:09 p.m.

11:09 p.m.
Just saves reviewing whitespace and formatting changes on PRs
11:13 p.m.

WZNRXSN 2026-02-17 12:32 a.m.
Hi https://github.com/zjs81/meshcore-open/pull/179 It's my Pr (edited)
Correct Chinese translation by MGJ520 · Pull Request #179 · zjs81...
Correct Chinese translation
1
RocketMan
I’ve been working on meshcore-open, an MIT open-source MeshCore client, and I wanted to share what it currently does in case it’s useful or interesting to others here. At a high level, it’s a local-first MeshCore interface that lets you interact with the mesh without relying on the internet. It supports encrypted direct messages and public channels, keeps local conversation history, and tracks contacts with last-seen timestamps. Messages support replies and reactions so conversations are easier to follow on busy meshes. On the mesh side, you can see routing paths, and how messages move through the network. Routes can rotate automatically or be overridden manually, and nearby nodes are discovered dynamically. There’s also a map view that shows live node locations when available, with filtering by node type and time range. You can share GPS locations or custom markers, use offline map tiles in remote areas. For device interaction, it connects over BLE (NUS) to MeshCore radios, allowing you to configure radio parameters, power settings, and network options, monitor battery state with chemistry-aware voltage curves. If you’re running repeaters, there’s a repeater hub that exposes stats, connected clients, traffic data, and remote management tools, including CLI access over the mesh itself. It’s still evolving and definitely has some rough edges, but the goal is to make an open source app that every one can freely use and modify. Feedback is welcome https://github.com/zjs81/meshcore-open

1
ded (SalishMesh)
I would love that, and we can stop using codes and one gif provider. Or at least have the option to choose until we get a proper way to send them
WZNRXSN 2026-03-03 8:39 a.m.
Hello ded (SalishMesh), thanks for your reply. I have to be honest: MeshCore Open is ahead of other open-source projects and official apps in some areas, especially with its advanced features in the message chat interface, which are really great. However, I find that MeshCore's UI design is far behind the official app, PocketMesh, and SAR. Do you have any plans to study UI design guidelines and work on improving these issues for MeshCore Open?
ded (SalishMesh) 2026-03-03 8:48 a.m.
There's no official app IMO, only a first app. But that aside, meshcore open is alpha. This is important to remember as its not even considered stable for use at this time. Much work is going on and then next release has a number of changes. But the overall UI will eventually be consistent within the app and we are always open to feedback about UI/UX improvements
1
2
1
8:48 a.m.
And you are right on the money in a way, all 3 of the projects mentioned are much more mature (have been around longer)

Jade 2026-03-11 10:48 a.m.
I love the icon. Please don't change it. It conveys what the app is about, isn't monchrome like so many app icons for this stuff are and is fun!
2
10:50 a.m.
Personal taste is subjective. I'd say only change it if there was a consensus to do so. But there are bigger issues than an app icon
2
10:51 a.m.
Also saying it's "very much AI-generated" is pretty useless since AI can generate plenty of boring monochrome icons. In fact it's best at that. This icon looks the opposite, purposeful, creative and intentional. (edited)
10:52 a.m.
In short any icon can look "AI generated".
10:53 a.m.
Too much of this stuff is monochrome and some shade of blue or grey. Like devs only live in a grey world. I feel MeshCore Open's icon stands out and conveys the fun of communicating on the mesh quite well so kudos to whoever designed it.
10:59 a.m.
I realize many live in a boring corporate world where every graphic is some monochrome boring corporate thing. Taking the fun and color out of life is the opposite of what I think about when I think about communicating with my friends on the mesh.
11:01 a.m.
tl;dr - The logo is Art. Art makes life more enjoyable.

2:34 p.m.

12:06 a.m.

11:07 a.m.

11:07 a.m.

11:51 a.m.

BarefootBandit 2026-03-24 5:08 p.m.

Uncle Lit 2026-03-29 12:38 p.m.
I am asking this for all companion applications. could you make sending messages in multi-byte option per channel and per contact? I imagine for a while even after all repeaters are updated to 1.14+, there will be channels you want to send with 1-byte to get 64 hops, and then there will be channels you want to send with 2- or 3-byte to disembogue repeater IDs and fewer hops. Another example: as a regional mesh updating their repeaters to 1.14+, I may want to create a #multibytepublic hashtag channel to test multibyte with others in public. We can do this to test 1.14 repeater firmware take rate and coverage. We would need to send only multibyte messages in #multibyte_public, but keep regular public channel in 1-byte path hash.
2
2
Uncle Lit
I am asking this for all companion applications. could you make sending messages in multi-byte option per channel and per contact? I imagine for a while even after all repeaters are updated to 1.14+, there will be channels you want to send with 1-byte to get 64 hops, and then there will be channels you want to send with 2- or 3-byte to disembogue repeater IDs and fewer hops. Another example: as a regional mesh updating their repeaters to 1.14+, I may want to create a #multibytepublic hashtag channel to test multibyte with others in public. We can do this to test 1.14 repeater firmware take rate and coverage. We would need to send only multibyte messages in #multibyte_public, but keep regular public channel in 1-byte path hash.
RocketMan 2026-04-03 1:45 p.m.
Thats a good idea. Yeah I'll add that once I flush out the multi-byte stuff
2

Roobert 2026-04-07 3:46 p.m.
@RocketMan I've been looking for an app that has a battery chart like Mt has. Can you make it an option where you can track the battery graph of the companion or repeater every minute or 5 min? It would be insanely helpful for testing new solar builds. Right now I have to load them with Mt for a bit then take it down and flash mc after j feel comfortable with the performance. Attached is an example of the Mt battery graph
3:46 p.m.
Roobert

Roobert 2026-04-07 3:47 p.m.
@RocketMan
3:47 p.m.

ded (SalishMesh) 2026-04-07 4:31 p.m.
this information is polled so it would require you to be connected to the companion at all times, and it would then grab the status from the repeater. doing this for all contacts is obvs bad. it would be possible once telemetry is complete we could add an auto fetch system on a per contact basis
4:33 p.m.
you can watch progress here https://github.com/zjs81/meshcore-open/issues/330
[Feature Request] Client and sensor nodes telemetry tab · Issue #3...
The proprietary Meshcore client has the option to request and display device telemetry of client devices, it would be a nice addition to have a similar tab in meshcore-open. My specific use case be...
4:33 p.m.
but it is not yet being worked on
4:36 p.m.

Pixelguy 2026-04-16 4:26 a.m.
Are there any downsides to using the MC open app in parallel to the "official" from liam? Any known sync problems for example? (edited)
Pixelguy
Are there any downsides to using the MC open app in parallel to the "official" from liam? Any known sync problems for example? (edited)
clauwn - BSmesh.de 2026-04-16 4:58 a.m.
there shouldn't be any problems; like mc open doesn't change any settings when connecting. you won't be able to use both at the exact same time though, you have to disconnect from one to use the other. the only downside might be that the discovered contacts would get split between the two apps, as they are only recorded while a client/app is connected to the node.
1
ded (SalishMesh) 2026-04-16 10:18 a.m.
The messages as well
10:18 a.m.
Which ever syncs the messages keeps them
10:18 a.m.
I use both all the time when testing though

Mike's Allotment 2026-04-16 8:05 p.m.
The app is now available in the Google Play store in the UK - just been playing and I have to say I'm super impressed . Great work I have however found a couple of issues... When you post a message to a RoomServer the Sender name for the message is shown as the name of the Room Server - not the name of the Companion that posted the message. Also - the posted messages are missing the first few bytes of every message. Is there a separate GitHub repository for raising issues like this? (edited)
1
8:09 p.m.
Actually... It's worse than I thought - messages posted to a Room Server aren't actually making it into the Room Server at all... the app seems to show the messages having been sent, but they are not actually sent to the Room.
